description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, featuring a prominent front panel. The front face displays a horizontal frequency indicator window above a series of vertical speaker grilles, flanked by two large round knobs and two smaller knobs, likely for tuning and volume control. The wood casing has a worn, slightly textured finish, suggesting age and frequent use. The back panel is perforated with diamond-shaped ventilation holes and includes a single rotary control and several small ports, typical of mid-20th-century radio designs. The sides are plain, showing the wood grain and subtle wear. The overall appearance is classic and utilitarian, evoking a sense of nostalgia and durability.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a frequency indicator, vertical speaker grille, and multiple control knobs on the front. The casing is worn and textured, with a perforated back panel for ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the frequency indicator window, vertical speaker grille, and four control knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain, textured wooden surface with rounded front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a perforated metal panel with ventilation holes, a rotary control, and small ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wooden surface, mirroring the left side.
